drafts.title: How pool leak detection works in Fair Play, SC
drafts.body:
# How pool leak detection works in Fair Play, SC

Aquatrace Swimming Pool Leak Detection uses a structured inspection process so pool owners can separate normal evaporation from actual water loss.

A recent completed job in Fair Play, SC followed the same evidence-first workflow: confirm reported loss, inspect structure and plumbing systems, document findings, and leave the owner with a clear next step.

Main drain cover documentation completed.

For pool owners in Fair Play and Lake Hartwell, the useful lesson is simple: measure daily loss, compare it against evaporation, and get a professional test when the numbers do not line up.

sources.label: Native completed job Receipt leak detection closeout
Native content media phase0-1783556748439-dc618248-media

contentQueueTurn.answer:
2 drafts waiting on approval, both from the Fair Play leak detection job:

- **GBP post** – "Aquatrace Swimming Pool Leak Detection: completed leak detection in Fair Play, SC" — covers the visit, main drain cover documentation, and references 1 field photo.
- **Article** – "How pool leak detection works in Fair Play, SC" — explains the inspection process using that same job as the example.

Both are tied to the same job and photo, and publishing is currently deferred until approved.
